signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Inflation
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.70 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage sustains the bid as US CPI beats expectations, reinforcing a hawkish Fed outlook. Despite Middle East tensions and Trump's oil supply headlines, JPY safe-haven demand remains absent with VIX at 18.3 (normal volatility). JPY COT LF net short -61,340 (crowded carry) faces limited relief as the USD/JPY vs US 10Y correlation (corr=+0.597 vs norm=+0.150, z=-2.19σ) breaks, suggesting JPY is failing to track yield pullbacks. Carry dominates until VIX > 25.

signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 113.89 — G8 bilateral spread of 0.866% confirms AUD outperformance against a weak JPY. The +360bp carry premium (RBA 4.35% vs BoJ 0.75%) remains the primary structural driver as RBA maintains its hiking stance while BoJ holds. Retail sentiment is 84% short, diverging from the COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long), signaling a potential retail capitulation risk if the carry-driven uptrend extends. Watch for BoJ verbal intervention if JPY weakness accelerates.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Technical + Inflation + Correlation
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1734 — price breaks below the 200-hour MA (1.1736) following a hot US CPI report that strengthens the USD DXY (+0.42%). EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation (corr=+0.706 vs norm=-0.112, z=+1.65σ) indicates EUR is currently trading as a proxy for European equity sentiment rather than its own macro fundamentals. With a 175bp carry deficit vs the Fed, the technical break shifts the short-term bias toward further downside. Watch for a sustained close below 1.1730 to confirm the trend shift.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5945 — COT LF net short -19,294 (long% 18.5%) shows specs are heavily fading the RBNZ despite a hawkish market bias and a 2.25% policy rate. With the RBNZ meeting on 27 May signaling a potential hike, the crowded spec short position creates significant short-squeeze risk if the central bank delivers a hawkish surprise. The 150bp Fed-RBNZ carry spread currently favors USD, but positioning extremes suggest the downside is becoming exhausted. Watch for RBNZ policy guidance to trigger a long-unwind or short-squeeze.
